1. List the contents of `packages/client-generator-js/` and `packages/client-generator-ts/` directories

Focus on understanding: how a generator is structured, what the entry point looks like, how it communicates with the Prisma CLI, and what build setup is needed.

input.description: Explore monorepo package setup and workspace config
input.prompt:
Explore the Prisma monorepo at the current working directory. I need to understand the monorepo setup for creating a new package. Specifically:

1. Read the root `package.json` to understand workspace scripts and Node/pnpm requirements
2. Read `pnpm-workspace.yaml` to see how packages are discovered
3. Read `turbo.json` to understand build pipeline
4. Read `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` (at the root) - specifically the `compilerOptions.paths` section which needs to be updated for new packages
5. Read `.github/workflows/test-template.yml` to understand how packages are added to CI
6. Look at a simple/small existing package (e.g., `packages/client-common/` or `packages/client-runtime-utils/`) and read its:
   - `package.json`
   - `tsconfig.json`
   - `tsconfig.build.json`
   - `helpers/build.ts`
   - `src/index.ts` (or main entry)
7. Read `packages/client-generator-registry/src/index.ts` to understand the generator registry

I want to understand the minimal setup needed for a new TypeScript generator package in this monorepo.

input.description: Explore DMMF types and generator protocol details
input.prompt:
Explore the Prisma monorepo at the current working directory. I need to understand the generator protocol and DMMF (Data Model Meta Format) types for building a new generator that creates a REST API client. Specifically:

1. Look in `packages/generator-helper/src/` - read all source files to understand GeneratorOptions, DMMF types, and the generator handler API
2. Look in `packages/dmmf/src/` - read the DMMF type definitions (especially model, field, enum definitions)
3. Look at how `packages/client-generator-ts/src/` uses DMMF to generate code - especially:
   - How it accesses `options.dmmf.datamodel.models`
   - How it handles fields, types, relations
   - Any file generation utilities it uses
4. Check `packages/ts-builders/src/` to understand the TypeScript code generation API
5. Look at `packages/client-generator-js/src/TSClient/` to understand patterns for generating TypeScript interfaces and methods

I want to understand:
- What DMMF data is available to a generator (model names, fields, types, relations)
- How to use `@prisma/generator-helper` to implement the generator protocol
- How to use `@prisma/ts-builders` for code generation (if appropriate)
- What field types exist and how they map to TypeScript types

1	name: CI Template job
2	
3	on:
4	  workflow_call:
5	    inputs:
6	      reason:
7	        description: |
8	          Reason why this workflow was called. Could be "buildpulse" for
9	          flaky tests detection or "daily-test" for scheduled full builds,
10	          "CI" for normal pushes/PRs
11	        type: string
12	        required: false
13	        default: CI
14	      jobTimeout:
15	        description: Timeout for the jobs. Default is 35 minutes
16	        type: number
17	        default: 35
18	        required: false
19	      jobsToRun:
20	        description: Jobs to run. See .github/workflows/scripts/detect-jobs-to-run.js script
21	        type: string
22	        default: '-all-'
23	        required: false
24	      pnpmVersion:
25	        description: pnpm package manager version
26	        type: string
27	        default: '10'
28	      engineHash:
29	        description: Customly-built engine hash to use
30	        type: string
31	        required: false
32	      driverAdapterTestJobTimeout:
33	        description: Timeout for driver adapter tests. Default is 60 minutes
34	        type: number
35	        default: 60
36	        required: false
37	    secrets:
38	      BUILDPULSE_ACCESS_KEY_ID:
39	        required: false
40	      BUILDPULSE_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY:
41	        required: false
42	      BOT_TOKEN:
43	        required: false
44	
45	env:
46	  HAS_BUILDPULSE_SECRETS: ${{ secrets.BUILDPULSE_ACCESS_KEY_ID != '' && secrets.BUILDPULSE_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY != '' }}
47	  PRISMA_TELEMETRY_INFORMATION: 'prisma test.yml'
48	  # To hide "Update available 0.0.0 -> x.x.x"
49	  PRISMA_HIDE_UPDATE_MESSAGE: true
50	  FUNCTIONAL_TEST_OPTIONS: --detectOpenHandles
51	
52	concurrency:
53	  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
54	  # Don't cancel if called from buildpulse workflow
55	  cancel-in-progress: ${{ inputs.reason != 'buildpulse' }}
56	
57	jobs:
58	  #
59	  # Linting
60	  #
61	  lint:
62	    name: Lint
63	    timeout-minutes: ${{ inputs.jobTimeout }}
64	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
65	
66	    steps:
67	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
68	
69	      - name: Install & build
70	        uses: ./.github/actions/setup
71	        with:
72	          node-version: '20.19.0'
73	          engine-hash: ${{ inputs.engineHash }}
74	          skip-tsc: false
75	
76	      # https://github.com/actions/toolkit/blob/master/docs/commands.md#problem-matchers
77	      # Matchers are added in setup-node
78	      # https://github.com/actions/setup-node/blob/bacd6b4b3ac3127b28a1e1920c23bf1c2cadbb85/src/main.ts#L54-L60
79	      - name: Disable GitHub annotations from linting
80	        run: |
81	          echo "::remove-matcher owner=eslint-compact::"
82	          echo "::remove-matcher owner=eslint-stylish::"
83	
84	      - name: Run eslint
85	        run: pnpm run lint
86	        working-directory: ./
87	
88	      - name: Run prettier-check
89	        run: pnpm run prettier-check
90	        working-directory: ./
91	
92	      - name: Run check-engines-override
93	        run: pnpm run check-engines-override
94	        working-directory: ./
95	
96	      - name: Run pnpm audit (production dependencies only)
97	        run: pnpm audit --prod
98

3	import { enginesVersion } from '@prisma/engines-version'
4	import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
5	import { BuiltInProvider, parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'
6	
7	import { version as clientVersion } from '../package.json'
8	import { generateClient } from './generateClient'
9	import { resolvePrismaClient } from './resolvePrismaClient'
10	
11	type PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {
12	  shouldResolvePrismaClient?: boolean
13	  shouldInstallMissingPackages?: boolean
14	  runtimePath?: string
15	}
16	
17	// const MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING = `\
18	// ${yellow('Warning:')} You did not specify an output path for your \`generator\` in schema.prisma. \
19	// This behavior is deprecated and will no longer be supported in Prisma 7.0.0. To learn more \
20	// visit https://pris.ly/cli/output-path`
21	
22	export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
23	  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs
24	
25	  #shouldResolvePrismaClient: boolean
26	  #runtimePath?: string
27	  #cachedPrismaClientPath: string | undefined
28	
29	  constructor({ shouldResolvePrismaClient = true, runtimePath }: PrismaClientJsGeneratorOptions = {}) {
30	    this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient = shouldResolvePrismaClient
31	    this.#runtimePath = runtimePath
32	  }
33	
34	  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
35	    // TODO: warning disabled for now until we fixed issues around custom output paths - see ORM-976
36	    // if (!config.output) {
37	    //   console.warn(MISSING_CUSTOM_OUTPUT_PATH_WARNING)
38	    // }
39	
40	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is true, which is normally the case,
41	    // we find the default output path by resolving the path to the
42	    // `@prisma/client` package. While we resolve the absolute path to the
43	    // package itself, the generator will rewrite it to replace
44	    // `.../@prisma/client` with `.../.prisma/client`, as long as
45	    // `config.isCustomOutput` is false.
46	    //
47	    // If a custom output path is provided, then the default output path doesn't
48	    // matter and we could use a static value here, but that unfortunately
49	    // wouldn't allow us to avoid resolving the client directory and would only
50	    // delay that until the `generate` method call because we copy the runtime
51	    // files if a custom output path is used, and we also copy the WASM modules
52	    // regardles of the output path. Not copying the runtime files is a breaking
53	    // change, so we can only completely get rid of this behaviour in the new
54	    // generator.
55	    //
56	    // If `this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient` is false, which is the case when
57	    // using the JSON-RPC compatibility adapter for Prisma Studio, we should use
58	    // a static value here.
59	    const defaultOutput = this.#shouldResolvePrismaClient ? await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config) : '.prisma/client'
60	
61	    return {
62	      defaultOutput,
63	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
64	      version: clientVersion,
65	      requiresEngines: [],
66	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
67	    }
68	  }
69	
70	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
71	    const outputDir = parseEnvValue(options.generator.output!)
72	
73	    await generateClient({
74	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
75	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
76	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
77	      datasources: options.datasources,
78	      outputDir,
79	      copyRuntime: Boolean(options.generator.config.copyRuntime),
80	      copyRuntimeSourceMaps: Boolean(process.env.PRISMA_COPY_RUNTIME_SOURCEMAPS),
81	      runtimeSourcePath: await this.#getRuntimePath(options.generator),
82	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
83	      generator: options.generator,
84	      engineVersion: options.version,
85	      clientVersion,
86	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
87	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
88	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild),
89	    })
90	  }
91	
92	  async #getPrismaClientPath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
93	    if (this.#cachedPrismaClientPath) {
94	      return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
95	    }
96	
97	    this.#cachedPrismaClientPath = await resolvePrismaClient(path.dirname(config.sourceFilePath))
98	    return this.#cachedPrismaClientPath
99	  }
100	
101	  async #getRuntimePath(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<string> {
102	    if (this.#runtimePath) {
103	      return this.#runtimePath
104	    }
105	
106	    this.#runtimePath = path.join(await this.#getPrismaClientPath(config), 'runtime')
107	    return this.#runtimePath
108	  }
109	}
110	
111	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown): 'fast' | 'small' {
112	  if (value === undefined) {
113	    return 'fast'
114	  }
115	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
116	    return value
117	  }
118	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
119	}
120

13	const missingOutputErrorMessage = `An output path is required for the \`prisma-client\` generator. Please provide an output path in your schema file:
14	
15	${dim(`generator client {
16	  provider = "prisma-client"`)}
17	${green('  output   = "../src/generated"')}
18	${dim('}')}
19	
20	${bold('Note:')} the output path is relative to the schema directory.
21	`
22	
23	function getOutputPath(config: GeneratorConfig): string {
24	  if (!config.output) {
25	    throw new Error(missingOutputErrorMessage)
26	  }
27	  return parseEnvValue(config.output)
28	}

30	export class PrismaClientTsGenerator implements Generator {
31	  readonly name = 'prisma-client-ts'
32	
33	  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
34	    return Promise.resolve({
35	      defaultOutput: getOutputPath(config),
36	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
37	      version: clientVersion,
38	      requiresEngines: [],
39	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
40	    })
41	  }
42	
43	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
44	    const { config } = options.generator
45	    const outputDir = getOutputPath(options.generator)
46	    const tsconfig = getTsconfig(outputDir)?.config
47	
48	    const target = config.runtime !== undefined ? parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown(config.runtime) : 'nodejs'
49	
50	    const generatedFileExtension =
51	      config.generatedFileExtension !== undefined ? parseGeneratedFileExtension(config.generatedFileExtension) : 'ts'
52	
53	    const importFileExtension =
54	      config.importFileExtension !== undefined
55	        ? parseImportFileExtension(config.importFileExtension)
56	        : inferImportFileExtension({
57	            tsconfig,
58	            generatedFileExtension,
59	            target,
60	          })
61	
62	    const moduleFormat =
63	      config.moduleFormat !== undefined
64	        ? parseModuleFormatFromUnknown(config.moduleFormat)
65	        : inferModuleFormat({
66	            tsconfig,
67	            generatedFileExtension,
68	            importFileExtension,
69	          })
70	
71	    await generateClient({
72	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
73	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
74	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
75	      datasources: options.datasources,
76	      outputDir,
77	      runtimeBase: '@prisma/client/runtime',
78	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
79	      generator: options.generator,
80	      engineVersion: options.version,
81	      clientVersion,
82	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
83	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
84	      target,
85	      generatedFileExtension,
86	      importFileExtension,
87	      moduleFormat,
88	      tsNoCheckPreamble: true, // Set to false only during internal tests
89	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild, target),
90	    })
91	  }
92	}
93	
94	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown, target: RuntimeTargetInternal): 'fast' | 'small' {
95	  if (value === undefined) {
96	    // using the 'small' build for 'vercel-edge' target to fit within their free tier limits
97	    return target === 'vercel-edge' ? 'small' : 'fast'
98	  }
99	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
100	    return value
101	  }
102	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
103	}

37	export interface GenerateClientOptions {
38	  datamodel: string
39	  schemaPath: string
40	  /** Runtime path used in runtime/type imports */
41	  runtimeBase: string
42	  outputDir: string
43	  generator: GeneratorConfig
44	  dmmf: DMMF.Document
45	  datasources: DataSource[]
46	  binaryPaths: BinaryPaths
47	  engineVersion: string
48	  clientVersion: string
49	  activeProvider: ActiveConnectorType
50	  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
51	  target: RuntimeTargetInternal
52	  generatedFileExtension: GeneratedFileExtension
53	  importFileExtension: ImportFileExtension
54	  moduleFormat: ModuleFormat
55	  /** Include a "@ts-nocheck" comment at the top of all generated TS files */
56	  tsNoCheckPreamble: Boolean
57	  compilerBuild: 'fast' | 'small'
58	}

60	export interface FileMap {
61	  [name: string]: string | Buffer | FileMap
62	}

64	export interface BuildClientResult {
65	  fileMap: FileMap
66	  prismaClientDmmf: DMMF.Document
67	}

69	export function buildClient({
70	  schemaPath,
71	  runtimeBase,
72	  datamodel,
73	  binaryPaths,
74	  outputDir,
75	  generator,
76	  dmmf,
77	  datasources,
78	  engineVersion,
79	  clientVersion,
80	  activeProvider,
81	  typedSql,
82	  target,
83	  generatedFileExtension,
84	  importFileExtension,
85	  moduleFormat,
86	  tsNoCheckPreamble,
87	  compilerBuild,
88	}: O.Required<GenerateClientOptions, 'runtimeBase'>): BuildClientResult {
89	  // we define the basic options for the client generation
90	  const runtimeName = getRuntimeNameForTarget(target)
91	
92	  const outputName = generatedFileNameMapper(generatedFileExtension)
93	  const importName = importFileNameMapper(importFileExtension)
94	
95	  const clientOptions: TSClientOptions = {
96	    dmmf: getPrismaClientDMMF(dmmf),
97	    datasources,
98	    generator,
99	    binaryPaths,
100	    schemaPath,
101	    outputDir,
102	    runtimeBase,
103	    clientVersion,
104	    engineVersion,
105	    activeProvider,
106	    datamodel,
107	    edge: (['wasm-compiler-edge'] as RuntimeName[]).includes(runtimeName),
108	    runtimeName: runtimeName,
109	    target,
110	    generatedFileExtension,
111	    importFileExtension,
112	    moduleFormat,
113	    tsNoCheckPreamble,
114	    compilerBuild,
115	  }
116	
117	  const client = new TSClient(clientOptions)
118	
119	  let fileMap = client.generateClientFiles()
120	
121	  if (typedSql && typedSql.length > 0) {
122	    fileMap = {
123	      ...fileMap,
124	      ...buildTypedSql({
125	        dmmf,
126	        runtimeBase: getTypedSqlRuntimeBase(runtimeBase),
127	        runtimeName,
128	        queries: typedSql,
129	        outputName,
130	        importName,
131	      }),
132	    }
133	  }
134	
135	  fileMap = {
136	    ...fileMap,
137	    internal: {
138	      ...(fileMap.internal as FileMap),
139	      ...buildWasmFileMap({
140	        runtimeName,
141	        activeProvider,
142	        compilerBuild,
143	      }),
144	    },
145	  }
146	
147	  addPreambleToSourceFiles(fileMap, tsNoCheckPreamble)
148	
149	  return {
150	    fileMap, // a map of file names to their contents
151	    prismaClientDmmf: dmmf, // the DMMF document
152	  }
153	}
154	
155	// relativizes runtime import base for typed sql
156	// absolute path stays unmodified, relative goes up a level
157	function getTypedSqlRuntimeBase(runtimeBase: string) {
158	  if (!runtimeBase.startsWith('.')) {
159	    // absolute path
160	    return runtimeBase
161	  }
162	
163	  if (runtimeBase.startsWith('./')) {
164	    // replace ./ with ../
165	    return `.${runtimeBase}`
166	  }
167	
168	  return `../${runtimeBase}`
169	}

171	export async function generateClient(options: GenerateClientOptions): Promise<void> {
172	  const {
173	    datamodel,
174	    schemaPath,
175	    generator,
176	    dmmf,
177	    datasources,
178	    binaryPaths,
179	    clientVersion,
180	    engineVersion,
181	    activeProvider,
182	    typedSql,
183	    target,
184	    generatedFileExtension,
185	    importFileExtension,
186	    moduleFormat,
187	    tsNoCheckPreamble,
188	    compilerBuild,
189	  } = options
190	
191	  const { runtimeBase, outputDir } = await getGenerationDirs(options)
192	
193	  const { prismaClientDmmf, fileMap } = buildClient({
194	    datamodel,
195	    schemaPath,
196	    runtimeBase,
197	    outputDir,
198	    generator,
199	    dmmf,
200	    datasources,
201	    binaryPaths,
202	    clientVersion,
203	    engineVersion,
204	    activeProvider,
205	    typedSql,
206	    target,
207	    generatedFileExtension,
208	    importFileExtension,
209	    moduleFormat,
210	    tsNoCheckPreamble,
211	    compilerBuild,
212	  })
213	
214	  const denylistsErrors = validateDmmfAgainstDenylists(prismaClientDmmf)
215	
216	  if (denylistsErrors) {
217	    let message = `${bold(
218	      red('Error: '),
219	    )}The schema at "${schemaPath}" contains reserved keywords.\n       Rename the following items:`
220	
221	    for (const error of denylistsErrors) {
222	      message += '\n         - ' + error.message
223	    }
224	
225	    message += `\nTo learn more about how to rename models, check out https://pris.ly/d/naming-models`
226	
227	    throw new DenylistError(message)
228	  }
229	
230	  await deleteOutputDir(outputDir)
231	  await ensureDir(outputDir)
232	
233	  await writeFileMap(outputDir, fileMap)
234	}
235	
236	function writeFileMap(outputDir: string, fileMap: FileMap) {
237	  return Promise.all(
238	    Object.entries(fileMap).map(async ([fileName, content]) => {
239	      const absolutePath = path.join(outputDir, fileName)
240	      // The deletion of the file is necessary, so VSCode
241	      // picks up the changes.
242	      await fs.rm(absolutePath, { recursive: true, force: true })
243	      if (typeof content === 'string' || Buffer.isBuffer(content)) {
244	        // file
245	        await fs.writeFile(absolutePath, content)
246	      } else {
247	        // subdirectory
248	        await fs.mkdir(absolutePath)
249	        await writeFileMap(absolutePath, content)
250	      }
251	    }),
252	  )
253	}
254	
255	function validateDmmfAgainstDenylists(prismaClientDmmf: DMMF.Document): Error[] | null {
256	  const errorArray = [] as Error[]
257	
258	  const denylists = {
259	    // A copy of this list is also in prisma-engines. Any edit should be done in both places.
260	    // https://github.com/prisma/prisma-engines/blob/main/psl/parser-database/src/names/reserved_model_names.rs
261	    models: [
262	      // Reserved Prisma keywords
263	      'PrismaClient',
264	      'Prisma',
265	      // JavaScript keywords
266	      'async',
267	      'await',
268	      'break',
269	      'case',
270	      'catch',
271	      'class',
272	      'const',
273	      'continue',
274	      'debugger',
275	      'default',
276	      'delete',
277	      'do',
278	      'else',
279	      'enum',
280	      'export',
281	      'extends',
282	      'false',
283	      'finally',
284	      'for',
285	      'function',
286	      'if',
287	      'implements',
288	      'import',
289	      'in',
290	      'instanceof',
291	      'interface',
292	      'let',
293	      'new',
294	      'null',
295	      'package',
296	      'private',
297	      'protected',
298	      'public',
299	      'return',
300	      'super',
301	      'switch',
302	      'this',
303	      'throw',
304	      'true',
305	      'try',
306	      'using',
307	      'typeof',
308	      'var',
309	      'void',
310	      'while',
311	      'with',
312	      'yield',
313	    ],
314	    fields: ['AND', 'OR', 'NOT'],
315	    dynamic: [],
316	  }
317	
318	  if (prismaClientDmmf.datamodel.enums) {
319	    for (const it of prismaClientDmmf.datamodel.enums) {
320	      if (denylists.models.includes(it.name) || denylists.fields.includes(it.name)) {
321	        errorArray.push(Error(`"enum ${it.name}"`))
322	      }
323	    }
324	  }
325	
326	  if (prismaClientDmmf.datamodel.models) {
327	    for (const it of prismaClientDmmf.datamodel.models) {
328	      if (denylists.models.includes(it.name) || denylists.fields.includes(it.name)) {
329	        errorArray.push(Error(`"model ${it.name}"`))
330	      }
331	    }
332	  }
333	
334	  return errorArray.length > 0 ? errorArray : null
335	}

337	/**
338	 * Get all the directories involved in the generation process.
339	 */
340	async function getGenerationDirs({ runtimeBase, outputDir }: GenerateClientOptions) {
341	  const normalizedOutputDir = path.normalize(outputDir)
342	  const normalizedRuntimeBase = pathToPosix(runtimeBase)
343	
344	  const userPackageRoot = await packageUp({ cwd: path.dirname(normalizedOutputDir) })
345	  const userProjectRoot = userPackageRoot ? path.dirname(userPackageRoot) : process.cwd()
346	
347	  return {
348	    runtimeBase: normalizedRuntimeBase,
349	    outputDir: normalizedOutputDir,
350	    projectRoot: userProjectRoot,
351	  }
352	}
353	
354	function getRuntimeNameForTarget(target: RuntimeTargetInternal): RuntimeName {
355	  switch (target) {
356	    case 'nodejs':
357	    case 'deno':
358	      return 'client'
359	
360	    case 'workerd':
361	    case 'vercel-edge':
362	      return 'wasm-compiler-edge'
363	
364	    default:
365	      assertNever(target, 'Unknown runtime target')
366	  }
367	}
368	
369	async function deleteOutputDir(outputDir: string) {
370	  try {
371	    const files = await fs.readdir(outputDir)
372	
373	    if (files.length === 0) {
374	      return
375	    }
376	
377	    if (
378	      !files.includes('client.ts') &&
379	      !files.includes('client.mts') &&
380	      !files.includes('client.cts') &&
381	      !files.includes('client.d.ts') // for legacy js client
382	    ) {
383	      // Make sure users don't accidentally wipe their source code or home directory.
384	      throw new Error(
385	        `${outputDir} exists and is not empty but doesn't look like a generated Prisma Client. ` +
386	          'Please check your output path and remove the existing directory if you indeed want to generate the Prisma Client in that location.',
387	      )
388	    }
389	
390	    await Promise.allSettled(
391	      (
392	        await glob(
393	          [
394	            `${outputDir}/**/*.{js,ts,mts,cts,d.ts}`,
395	            `${outputDir}/**/*.wasm`,
396	            `${outputDir}/*.node`,
397	            `${outputDir}/{query,schema}-engine-*`,
398	            `${outputDir}/package.json`,
399	            `${outputDir}/**/*.prisma`,
400	          ],
401	          {
402	            followSymbolicLinks: false,
403	          },
404	        )
405	      ).map((file) => fs.unlink(file)),
406	    )
407	  } catch (error) {
408	    if ((error as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code !== 'ENOENT') {
409	      throw error
410	    }
411	  }
412	}

1	import path from 'node:path'
2	
3	import { BuildOptions } from './build'
4	
5	type AdapterEntry = {
6	  entry: string
7	  outfile: string
8	}
9	
10	export function createAdapterConfig(entries: AdapterEntry[]): BuildOptions[] {
11	  return entries.flatMap(({ entry, outfile }) => {
12	    const baseName = path.basename(outfile)
13	
14	    return [
15	      {
16	        name: `${baseName}-cjs`,
17	        format: 'cjs' as const,
18	        bundle: true,
19	        entryPoints: [entry],
20	        outfile,
21	        outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
22	        emitTypes: true,
23	      },
24	      {
25	        name: `${baseName}-esm`,
26	        format: 'esm' as const,
27	        bundle: true,
28	        entryPoints: [entry],
29	        outfile,
30	        outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
31	        emitTypes: true,
32	      },
33	    ]
34	  })
35	}
36	
37	export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }])
38	
39	export const unbundledConfig: BuildOptions[] = [
40	  {
41	    name: 'cjs',
42	    format: 'cjs',
43	    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
44	    outdir: 'dist',
45	    outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
46	    emitTypes: true,
47	  },
48	  {
49	    name: 'esm',
50	    format: 'esm',
51	    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
52	    outdir: 'dist',
53	    outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
54	    emitTypes: true,
55	  },
56	]

1	import type { O } from 'ts-toolbelt'
2	
3	import { DMMFHelper } from '../dmmf'
4	import { generatedFileNameMapper, importFileNameMapper } from '../file-extensions'
5	import type { FileMap } from '../generateClient'
6	import { GenerateClientOptions } from '../generateClient'
7	import { GenericArgsInfo } from '../GenericsArgsInfo'
8	import { createBrowserFile } from './file-generators/BrowserFile'
9	import { createClassFile } from './file-generators/ClassFile'
10	import { createClientFile } from './file-generators/ClientFile'
11	import { createCommonInputTypeFiles } from './file-generators/CommonInputTypesFile'
12	import { createEnumsFile } from './file-generators/EnumsFile'
13	import { createModelFile } from './file-generators/ModelFile'
14	import { createModelsFile } from './file-generators/ModelsFile'
15	import { createPrismaNamespaceBrowserFile } from './file-generators/PrismaNamespaceBrowserFile'
16	import { createPrismaNamespaceFile } from './file-generators/PrismaNamespaceFile'
17	import { GenerateContext } from './GenerateContext'
18	
19	export type RuntimeName = 'wasm-compiler-edge' | 'client' | (string & {})
20	
21	export type TSClientOptions = O.Required<GenerateClientOptions, 'runtimeBase'> & {
22	  /** The name of the runtime bundle to use */
23	  runtimeName: RuntimeName
24	  /** When we are generating an edge-compatible client */
25	  edge: boolean
26	}
27	
28	export class TSClient {
29	  protected readonly dmmf: DMMFHelper
30	  protected readonly genericsInfo: GenericArgsInfo
31	
32	  constructor(protected readonly options: TSClientOptions) {
33	    this.dmmf = new DMMFHelper(options.dmmf)
34	    this.genericsInfo = new GenericArgsInfo(this.dmmf)
35	  }
36	
37	  generateClientFiles(): FileMap {
38	    const context = new GenerateContext({
39	      dmmf: this.dmmf,
40	      genericArgsInfo: this.genericsInfo,
41	      runtimeBase: this.options.runtimeBase,
42	      runtimeImport: `${this.options.runtimeBase}/${this.options.runtimeName}`,
43	      outputFileName: generatedFileNameMapper(this.options.generatedFileExtension),
44	      importFileName: importFileNameMapper(this.options.importFileExtension),
45	      generator: this.options.generator,
46	      provider: this.options.activeProvider,
47	    })
48	
49	    const modelNames = Object.values(context.dmmf.typeAndModelMap)
50	      .filter((model) => context.dmmf.outputTypeMap.model[model.name])
51	      .map((model) => model.name)
52	
53	    const modelsFileMap: FileMap = modelNames.reduce((acc, modelName) => {
54	      acc[context.outputFileName(modelName)] = createModelFile(context, modelName)
55	      return acc
56	    }, {})
57	
58	    return {
59	      [context.outputFileName('client')]: createClientFile(context, this.options),
60	      [context.outputFileName('browser')]: createBrowserFile(context, this.options),
61	      [context.outputFileName('enums')]: createEnumsFile(context),
62	      [context.outputFileName('commonInputTypes')]: createCommonInputTypeFiles(context),
63	      [context.outputFileName('models')]: createModelsFile(context, modelNames),
64	      models: modelsFileMap,
65	      internal: {
66	        [context.outputFileName('prismaNamespace')]: createPrismaNamespaceFile(context, this.options),
67	        [context.outputFileName('prismaNamespaceBrowser')]: createPrismaNamespaceBrowserFile(context),
68	        [context.outputFileName('class')]: createClassFile(context, this.options),
69	      },
70	    }
71	  }
72	}

1	import { ChokidarOptions, watch as createWatcher } from 'chokidar'
2	import * as esbuild from 'esbuild'
3	import { BuildContext } from 'esbuild'
4	import { writeFileSync } from 'fs'
5	import glob from 'globby'
6	import path from 'path'
7	
8	import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
9	import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
10	import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
11	import { map } from '../blaze/map'
12	import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'
13	import { pipe } from '../blaze/pipe'
14	import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
15	import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
16	import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
17	import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
18	import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'
19	
20	export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
21	export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
22	  name?: string
23	  emitTypes?: boolean
24	  emitMetafile?: boolean
25	  outbase?: never // we don't support this
26	}
27	
28	const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
29	  platform: 'node',
30	  target: 'ES2022',
31	  logLevel: 'error',
32	  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
33	  metafile: true,
34	} as const
35	
36	/**
37	 * Apply defaults to the original build options
38	 * @param options the original build options
39	 */
40	const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
41	  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
42	  format: 'cjs',
43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
44	  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
45	  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
46	    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
47	  }),
48	  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
49	  ...options,
50	  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
51	  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
58	    onErrorPlugin,
59	  ],
60	  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
61	})
62	
63	/**
64	 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * We only want to trigger the glob search once we are ready, and that is when
82	 * the previous build has finished. We get the build options from the deferred.
83	 */
84	function computeOptions(options: () => BuildOptions) {
85	  return options()
86	}
87	
88	/**
89	 * Extensions are not automatically by esbuild set for `options.outfile`. We
90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */
92	function addExtensionFormat(options: BuildOptions) {
93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`
97	  }
98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`
104	 */
105	function addDefaultOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path
207	function getOutFile(options: BuildOptions) {
208	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)
210	    const filename = path.basename(options.outfile)
211	
212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []
232	}

23	        } satisfies GeneratorRegistryEntry,

10	export function createAdapterConfig(entries: AdapterEntry[]): BuildOptions[] {
11	  return entries.flatMap(({ entry, outfile }) => {
12	    const baseName = path.basename(outfile)
13	
14	    return [
15	      {
16	        name: `${baseName}-cjs`,
17	        format: 'cjs' as const,
18	        bundle: true,
19	        entryPoints: [entry],
20	        outfile,
21	        outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
22	        emitTypes: true,
23	      },
24	      {
25	        name: `${baseName}-esm`,
26	        format: 'esm' as const,
27	        bundle: true,
28	        entryPoints: [entry],
29	        outfile,
30	        outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
31	        emitTypes: true,
32	      },
33	    ]
34	  })
35	}

1	import { ChokidarOptions, watch as createWatcher } from 'chokidar'
2	import * as esbuild from 'esbuild'
3	import { BuildContext } from 'esbuild'
4	import { writeFileSync } from 'fs'
5	import glob from 'globby'
6	import path from 'path'
7	
8	import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
9	import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
10	import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
11	import { map } from '../blaze/map'
12	import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'
13	import { pipe } from '../blaze/pipe'
14	import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
15	import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
16	import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
17	import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
18	import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'
19	
20	export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
21	export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
22	  name?: string
23	  emitTypes?: boolean
24	  emitMetafile?: boolean
25	  outbase?: never // we don't support this
26	}
27	
28	const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
29	  platform: 'node',
30	  target: 'ES2022',
31	  logLevel: 'error',
32	  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
33	  metafile: true,
34	} as const
35	
36	/**
37	 * Apply defaults to the original build options
38	 * @param options the original build options
39	 */
40	const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
41	  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
42	  format: 'cjs',
43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
44	  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
45	  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
46	    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
47	  }),
48	  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
49	  ...options,
50	  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
51	  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
58	    onErrorPlugin,
59	  ],
60	  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
61	})
62	
63	/**
64	 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * We only want to trigger the glob search once we are ready, and that is when
82	 * the previous build has finished. We get the build options from the deferred.
83	 */
84	function computeOptions(options: () => BuildOptions) {
85	  return options()
86	}
87	
88	/**
89	 * Extensions are not automatically by esbuild set for `options.outfile`. We
90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */
92	function addExtensionFormat(options: BuildOptions) {
93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`
97	  }
98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`
104	 */
105	function addDefaultOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path
207	function getOutFile(options: BuildOptions) {
208	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)
210	    const filename = path.basename(options.outfile)
211	
212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []
232	}

## Monorepo Setup Analysis

I've explored the Prisma monorepo to understand the minimal setup needed for creating a new TypeScript generator package. Here's what you need to know:

### 1. **Root Configuration Files**

**package.json** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775470299165-05w3kq/package.json`)
- **Node/pnpm requirements**: Node `^20.19 || ^22.12 || >=24.0`, pnpm `>=10.15 <11`
- **Key workspace scripts**:
  - `build`: `turbo build` (build all packages)
  - `dev`: `turbo dev` (watch mode)
  - `test`: runs CI publish script with tests
  - `lint` and `lint-fix`: ESLint across monorepo
  - `format`: Prettier formatting
- Uses `pnpm` workspaces with `peerDependencyRules` configuration

**pnpm-workspace.yaml** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775470299165-05w3kq/pnpm-workspace.yaml`)
- Discovers packages via: `packages: ['packages/*']`
- Only built dependencies are: `@swc/core`, `better-sqlite3`, `esbuild`, `sharp`, `sqlite3`, `workerd`, `yarn`

**turbo.json** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775470299165-05w3kq/turbo.json`)
- `build` task depends on `^build` (transitive dependencies)
- Watches inputs: `$TURBO_DEFAULT$` and `$TURBO_ROOT$/helpers/**/*.ts`
- Output directories: `dist/**`, `generator-build/**`, `runtime/**`
- Supports `DEV` and `MINIFY` environment variables

### 2. **TypeScript Configuration**

**tsconfig.build.bundle.json** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775470299165-05w3kq/tsconfig.build.bundle.json`)
- **Critical section - `compilerOptions.paths`**: Maps package names to source directories
- Existing pattern: `"@prisma/<package-name>": ["./packages/<package-name>/src"]`
- **You must add an entry here** for any new package. Example:
  ```json
  "@prisma/my-new-generator": ["./packages/my-new-generator/src"]
  ```

**tsconfig.json** (root) and **tsconfig.build.regular.json**
- Root extends `tsconfig.utils.typecheck.json`
- Build config uses ES2022 target, commonjs module, DOM lib
- `emitDeclarationOnly: true` for declaration-only builds
- Excludes: `dist`, `build`, `fixtures`, `node_modules`, `.prisma/client`, test directories

### 3. **Minimal Package Structure**

Looking at existing simple packages like `client-common` and `client-runtime-utils`:

**Directory layout**:
```
packages/my-generator/
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
├── tsconfig.build.json
├── src/
│   └── index.ts
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts
└── README.md
```

**package.json** template:
```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/my-generator",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index.js"
      },
      "import": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index.mjs"
      }
    }
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git",
    "directory": "packages/my-generator"
  },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build",
    "test": "vitest run"
  },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

**tsconfig.json**:
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

**tsconfig.build.json**:
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

**helpers/build.ts** (minimal):
```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { unbundledConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(unbundledConfig)
```

**src/index.ts** (entry point):
- Just export your main functionality
- Export re-exports pattern: `export * from './MyGenerator'`

### 4. **Build System Details**

The build system uses **esbuild** with custom helpers (`/helpers/compile/`)

**Build configs** (`helpers/compile/configs.ts`):
- `unbundledConfig`: Builds all `.ts` files in `src/` to both `.js` (CJS) and `.mjs` (ESM)
- `adapterConfig`: For bundled single-entry packages (entry → `dist/index`)
- Both emit types (`emitTypes: true`) and use `emitMetafile`

The build pipeline:
1. Uses esbuild with `tsconfig.build.json`
2. Generates both CJS and ESM outputs
3. Emits TypeScript declaration files
4. Supports watch mode via `DEV=true` env variable
5. Resolves path aliases from tsconfig

### 5. **Generator Pattern** (from `client-generator-registry`)

If creating a **generator package** specifically:

**GeneratorRegistry** pattern:
```typescript
// registry.ts
export class GeneratorRegistry {
  #generators = new Map<string, Generator>()
  add(generator: Generator) { ... }
  addAliased(name: string, generator: Generator) { ... }
  toInternal(): IGeneratorRegistry { ... }
}

// default.ts
export const defaultRegistry = new GeneratorRegistry()
defaultRegistry.add(new MyGenerator())
```

The registry imports:
- `@prisma/generator` - base Generator type
- `@prisma/internals` - GeneratorRegistry interface
- Individual generator implementations

**Dependencies typically needed**:
- `@prisma/generator` (workspace)
- `@prisma/internals` (workspace)
- `@prisma/dmmf` (workspace) - for schema/model access

### 6. **CI/CD Integration**

**GitHub workflow** (`.github/workflows/test-template.yml`):
- No explicit per-package job configuration needed
- Build/test jobs use:
  - `./.github/actions/setup` - handles node/pnpm setup and initial build
  - `pnpm run test` for unit tests
  - Tests run in matrix across multiple Node versions (20.19.0, 22.12.0, 24)
- Package tests are autodiscovered - just ensure your `package.json` has a `test` script

### 7. **Checklist for New Generator Package**

To create a new TypeScript generator package:

1. Create directory: `packages/my-generator/`
2. Copy template files (package.json, tsconfig variants, helpers/build.ts)
3. **Add path mapping** to `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`:
   ```json
   "@prisma/my-generator": ["./packages/my-generator/src"]
   ```
4. Create `src/index.ts` with exports
5. Add implementation files in `src/`
6. If it's a generator, implement `Generator` interface from `@prisma/generator`
7. Run `pnpm install` (pnpm automatically detects new workspace package)
8. Test: `pnpm build` and `pnpm test`

**Key dependencies pattern**:
- Use `workspace:*` for internal Prisma packages
- Keep external dependencies minimal
- Non-dev dependencies become external (not bundled)
- Dev dependencies get bundled if using `adapterConfig`

The monorepo uses **Turbo for orchestration**, **esbuild for compilation**, and **pnpm workspaces for dependency management**. The build system is quite standardized - most packages follow the exact same pattern.

Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the Prisma generator architecture. Let me create a detailed report.

## Prisma Generator Architecture Exploration Report

I've completed a thorough exploration of the Prisma monorepo's generator package structure. Here's what I found:

---

## 1. GENERATOR PACKAGES DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

### `/packages/client-generator-js/`
```
client-generator-js/
├── package.json          (entry point: dist/index.js)
├── tsconfig.json         (extends root tsconfig)
├── tsconfig.build.json   (ESM/CJS build output to dist/)
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts         (main exports: PrismaClientJsGenerator, generateClient, etc.)
│   ├── generator.ts     (Generator class implementation)
│   ├── generateClient.ts (client generation logic)
│   ├── TSClient/        (TypeScript generation for client classes)
│   └── utils/           (utility functions)
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts         (calls helpers/compile/build with adapterConfig)
└── tests/
```

### `/packages/client-generator-ts/`
```
client-generator-ts/
├── package.json          (entry point: dist/index.js)
├── tsconfig.json         (extends root tsconfig)
├── tsconfig.build.json   (ESM/CJS build output to dist/)
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts         (main exports: PrismaClientTsGenerator, generateClient)
│   ├── generator.ts     (Generator class with TypeScript-specific options)
│   ├── generateClient.ts (client generation with TS configuration)
│   ├── file-extensions.ts
│   ├── module-format.ts
│   ├── runtime-targets.ts
│   ├── TSClient/        (27 files: Args, Model, Count, Enum, etc.)
│   ├── typedSql/        (typed SQL query support)
│   └── utils/           (utility functions)
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts         (calls helpers/compile/build with adapterConfig)
└── tests/
```

---

## 2. GENERATOR PROTOCOL INTERFACE

**Location**: `/packages/generator/src/`

The core generator protocol is defined in the `@prisma/generator` package:

### `Generator` Interface
```typescript
export type Generator = {
  readonly name: string
  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
  generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
}
```

### Key Configuration Types
- **GeneratorConfig**: Schema file configuration (name, output, provider, binaryTargets, previewFeatures, sourceFilePath)
- **GeneratorOptions**: Runtime data passed to generator (generator, otherGenerators, schemaPath, dmmf, datasources, datamodel, version, binaryPaths, typedSql)
- **GeneratorManifest**: Metadata about the generator (defaultOutput, prettyName, version, requiresEngines, requiresEngineVersion)
- **DataSource**: Database connection info (name, provider, activeProvider, schemas, sourceFilePath)

---

## 3. GENERATOR HANDLER (Communication Protocol)

**Location**: `/packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts`

Implements JSON-RPC based communication with Prisma CLI:

```typescript
export interface Handler {
  onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<any>
  onManifest?(config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest | Promise<GeneratorManifest>
}

export function generatorHandler(handler: Handler): void
```

**Communication Flow**:
- CLI writes JSON-RPC requests to `stdin`
- Generator processes `generate` and `getManifest` methods
- Generator writes JSON-RPC responses to `stderr`
- Uses readline interface for line-by-line JSON parsing

---

## 4. EXISTING GENERATOR IMPLEMENTATIONS

### A. `PrismaClientJsGenerator` (JavaScript)
**Location**: `/packages/client-generator-js/src/generator.ts`

```typescript
export class PrismaClientJsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = BuiltInProvider.PrismaClientJs
  
  async getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
}
```

**Features**:
- Resolves `@prisma/client` package path
- Supports custom output paths
- Generates JavaScript and type definition files
- Copies runtime files when needed
- Handles `copyRuntime` configuration

### B. `PrismaClientTsGenerator` (TypeScript)
**Location**: `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/generator.ts`

```typescript
export class PrismaClientTsGenerator implements Generator {
  readonly name = 'prisma-client-ts'
  
  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
}
```

**Features**:
- **Requires explicit output path** (throws error if missing)
- Parses `tsconfig.json` from output directory
- Configurable runtime targets: `nodejs` (default), `browser`, `vercel-edge`, etc.
- Configurable file extensions: `ts`, `js`, `mts`, `mjs`
- Configurable import extensions
- Configurable module format: `esm`, `cjs`, `auto`
- Includes TypeScript-specific code generation (TSClient)
- Generates typed SQL query support

---

## 5. ENTRY POINTS & EXPORTS

### client-generator-js/src/index.ts
```typescript
export { externalToInternalDmmf } from './externalToInternalDmmf'
export { generateClient, type GenerateClientOptions } from './generateClient'
export { PrismaClientJsGenerator } from './generator'
export { getDMMF } from './getDMMF'
export { dmmfToTypes } from './utils/types/dmmfToTypes'
```

### client-generator-ts/src/index.ts
```typescript

export { generateClient, type GenerateClientOptions } from './generateClient'
export { PrismaClientTsGenerator } from './generator'
export { getDMMF } from './getDMMF'
```

---

## 6. BUILD SETUP & CONFIGURATION

### Build System
**Primary Build Tool**: esbuild with custom wrapper

**Location**: `/helpers/compile/build.ts`

**Key Features**:
- Supports both bundled and unbundled outputs
- Dual format compilation: CommonJS + ESM
- Automatic TypeScript type generation
- File watching for development (`WATCH` env var)
- Metadata file emission
- Plugin system (fixImportsPlugin, resolvePathsPlugin, tscPlugin, onErrorPlugin)

### Adapter Configuration
**Location**: `/helpers/compile/configs.ts`

```typescript
export function createAdapterConfig(entries: AdapterEntry[]): BuildOptions[]
export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }])
export const unbundledConfig: BuildOptions[]
```

Creates dual builds (CJS + ESM) for each entry point:
- **CJS**: `dist/index.js`
- **ESM**: `dist/index.mjs`
- Both with TypeScript definitions (`.d.ts`, `.d.mts`)

### Generator Package Build Setup

Both generator packages use identical build setup:

**helpers/build.ts** (both packages):
```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(adapterConfig)
```

**package.json scripts**:
```json
{
  "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
  "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
  "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build",
  "test": "vitest run --testTimeout 30000"
}
```

---

## 7. GENERATOR REGISTRY

**Location**: `/packages/client-generator-registry/`

### Purpose
Central registry for discovering and loading all available generators.

### Key Classes

**GeneratorRegistry** (registry.ts):
```typescript
export class GeneratorRegistry {
  #generators = new Map<string, Generator>()
  add(generator: Generator): void
  addAliased(name: string, generator: Generator): void
  toInternal(): IGeneratorRegistry  // converts to internal format
}
```

**Default Registry** (default.ts):
```typescript
export const defaultRegistry = new GeneratorRegistry()
defaultRegistry.add(new PrismaClientJsGenerator())

const tsGenerator = new PrismaClientTsGenerator()
defaultRegistry.add(tsGenerator)
defaultRegistry.addAliased('prisma-client', tsGenerator)
```

---

## 8. DEPENDENCIES HIERARCHY

### Common Dependencies (Both Generators)
- `@prisma/client-common` - Shared client utilities
- `@prisma/debug` - Debug utilities
- `@prisma/dmmf` - DMMF type definitions
- `@prisma/engines-version` - Pinned engine version
- `@prisma/fetch-engine` - Engine binary fetching
- `@prisma/generator` - Base generator protocol
- `@prisma/get-platform` - Platform detection
- `@prisma/internals` - Internal utilities
- `@prisma/param-graph-builder` - Parameter graph building
- `@prisma/ts-builders` - TypeScript builder utilities
- `klona` - Deep cloning
- `pluralize` - Pluralization utility
- `ts-pattern` - Pattern matching
- `indent-string` - String indentation

### TS Generator Only
- `fast-glob` - Fast file globbing
- `get-tsconfig` - TypeScript config parsing

### Development
- `@types/pluralize` - Type definitions
- `@types/node` - Node.js types

---

## 9. PROTOCOL COMMUNICATION FLOW

```
┌─────────────────┐
│   Prisma CLI    │
└────────┬────────┘
         │
         │ JSON-RPC requests → stdin
         │ {"method":"getManifest",...}
         │ {"method":"generate",...}
         ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│   Generator Process (spawn as child)    │
│                                         │
│  generatorHandler({                     │
│    onManifest: () => manifest,          │
│    onGenerate: () => generateFiles()    │
│  })                                     │
│                                         │
│  Process.stdin → readline → JSON parse  │
│  → Handler methods → JSON serialize     │
│  → Process.stderr                       │
└────────┬────────────────────────────────┘
         │
         │ JSON-RPC responses ← stderr
         │ {"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":...}
         │
         ↓
┌─────────────────┐
│   Prisma CLI    │
└─────────────────┘
```

---

## 10. IMPLEMENTATION CHECKLIST FOR NEW GENERATOR

Based on the architecture, a new generator package should:

1. **Package Structure**:
   - `/src/index.ts` - Main entry point with exports
   - `/src/generator.ts` - Class implementing `Generator` interface
   - `/src/generateClient.ts` or similar - Core generation logic
   - `/helpers/build.ts` - References `/helpers/compile/build`
   - `/tsconfig.json` - Extends root tsconfig
   - `/tsconfig.build.json` - Extends root tsconfig.build.regular.json
   - `/package.json` - With standard scripts (build, dev, test)

2. **Generator Class Must**:
   - Implement `Generator` interface (name, getManifest, generate)
   - Export from index.ts
   - Be added to GeneratorRegistry in client-generator-registry/src/default.ts

3. **Build Configuration**:
   - Use shared esbuild setup via `adapterConfig`
   - Generate both CJS and ESM outputs
   - Emit type definitions
   - Export paths: `dist/index.js`, `dist/index.mjs`, `dist/index.d.ts`, `dist/index.d.mts`

4. **Dependencies**:
   - Core: `@prisma/generator`, `@prisma/dmmf`, `@prisma/internals`
   - Supporting: `@prisma/debug`, other as needed
   - Package versions from existing generators

5. **Entry Point Handler** (Optional):
   - If building a standalone generator tool, use `generatorHandler` from `@prisma/generator-helper`
   - Implement Handler interface (onGenerate, onManifest)

---

## KEY FILES REFERENCE

| Purpose | File Path |
|---------|-----------|
| Generator Base Protocol | `/packages/generator/src/generator.ts` |
| Configuration Types | `/packages/generator/src/configuration.ts` |
| JSON-RPC Handler | `/packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts` |
| Build System | `/helpers/compile/build.ts` |
| Build Config Template | `/helpers/compile/configs.ts` |
| JS Generator Example | `/packages/client-generator-js/src/generator.ts` |
| TS Generator Example | `/packages/client-generator-ts/src/generator.ts` |
| Generator Registry | `/packages/client-generator-registry/src/registry.ts` |
| Registry Default Setup | `/packages/client-generator-registry/src/default.ts` |

---

This comprehensive structure shows that Prisma generators are well-architected with clear separation of concerns, a standardized protocol, and consistent build/package setup across implementations. New generators can follow this pattern for seamless integration into the ecosystem.

## Prisma Generator Architecture Exploration Report

I've completed a thorough exploration of the Prisma monorepo's generator package structure. Here's what I found:

---

## 1. GENERATOR PACKAGES DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

### `/packages/client-generator-js/`

```
├── package.json          (entry point: dist/index.js)
├── tsconfig.json         (extends root tsconfig)
├── tsconfig.build.json   (ESM/CJS build output to dist/)
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts         (main exports: PrismaClientJsGenerator, generateClient, etc.)
│   ├── generator.ts     (Generator class implementation)
│   ├── generateClient.ts (client generation logic)
│   ├── TSClient/        (TypeScript generation for client classes)
│   └── utils/           (utility functions)
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts         (calls helpers/compile/build with adapterConfig)
└── tests/
```

### `/packages/client-generator-ts/`
```
client-generator-ts/
├── package.json          (entry point: dist/index.js)
├── tsconfig.json         (extends root tsconfig)

├── src/
│   ├── index.ts         (main exports: PrismaClientTsGenerator, generateClient)
│   ├── generator.ts     (Generator class with TypeScript-specific options)
│   ├── generateClient.ts (client generation with TS configuration)
│   ├── file-extensions.ts
│   ├── module-format.ts
│   ├── runtime-targets.ts
│   ├── TSClient/        (27 files: Args, Model, Count, Enum, etc.)
│   ├── typedSql/        (typed SQL query support)
│   └── utils/           (utility functions)
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts         (calls helpers/compile/build with adapterConfig)
└── tests/
```

---

## 2. GENERATOR PROTOCOL INTERFACE

**Location**: `/packages/generator/src/`

The core generator protocol is defined in the `@prisma/generator` package:

### `Generator` Interface
```typescript
export type Generator = {
  readonly name: string
  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest>
  generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void>
}
```

### Key Configuration Types
- **GeneratorConfig**: Schema file configuration (name, output, provider, binaryTargets, previewFeatures, sourceFilePath)
- **GeneratorOptions**: Runtime data passed to generator (generator, otherGenerators, schemaPath, dmmf, datasources, datamodel, version, binaryPaths, typedSql)
- **GeneratorManifest**: Metadata about the generator (defaultOutput, prettyName, version, requiresEngines, requiresEngineVersion)
- **DataSource**: Database connection info (name, provider, activeProvider, schemas, sourceFilePath)

---

## 3. GENERATOR HANDLER (Communication Protocol)

**Location**: `/packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts`

Implements JSON-RPC based communication with Prisma CLI:

```typescript
export interface Handler {
  onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<any>
  onManifest?(config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest | Promise<GeneratorManifest>
}

export function generatorHandler(handler: Handler): void
```

**Communication Flow**:
- CLI writes JSON-RPC requests to `stdin`
- Generator processes `generate` and `getManifest` methods
- Generator writes JSON-RPC responses to `stderr`
- Uses readline interface for line-by-line JSON parsing

### A. `PrismaClientJsGenerator` (JavaScript)
**Location**: `/packages/client-generator-js/src/generator.ts`

**Features**:

```
┌─────────────────┐

└────────┬────────┘
```

## 10. IMPLEMENTATION CHECKLIST FOR NEW GENERATOR

import { PrismaClientJsGenerator } from '@prisma/client-generator-js'
import { PrismaClientTsGenerator } from '@prisma/client-generator-ts'

import { GeneratorRegistry } from './registry'

export const defaultRegistry = new GeneratorRegistry()

defaultRegistry.add(new PrismaClientJsGenerator())

const tsGenerator = new PrismaClientTsGenerator()
defaultRegistry.add(tsGenerator)
defaultRegistry.addAliased('prisma-client', tsGenerator)

content.content:
1	import { enginesVersion } from '@prisma/engines-version'
2	import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
3	import { parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'
4	import { getTsconfig } from 'get-tsconfig'
5	import { bold, dim, green } from 'kleur/colors'
6	
7	import { version as clientVersion } from '../package.json'
8	import { inferImportFileExtension, parseGeneratedFileExtension, parseImportFileExtension } from './file-extensions'
9	import { generateClient } from './generateClient'
10	import { inferModuleFormat, parseModuleFormatFromUnknown } from './module-format'
11	import { parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown, RuntimeTargetInternal } from './runtime-targets'
12	
13	const missingOutputErrorMessage = `An output path is required for the \`prisma-client\` generator. Please provide an output path in your schema file:
14	
15	${dim(`generator client {
16	  provider = "prisma-client"`)}
17	${green('  output   = "../src/generated"')}
18	${dim('}')}
19	
20	${bold('Note:')} the output path is relative to the schema directory.
21	`
22	
23	function getOutputPath(config: GeneratorConfig): string {
24	  if (!config.output) {
25	    throw new Error(missingOutputErrorMessage)
26	  }
27	  return parseEnvValue(config.output)
28	}
29	
30	export class PrismaClientTsGenerator implements Generator {
31	  readonly name = 'prisma-client-ts'
32	
33	  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
34	    return Promise.resolve({
35	      defaultOutput: getOutputPath(config),
36	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
37	      version: clientVersion,
38	      requiresEngines: [],
39	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
40	    })
41	  }
42	
43	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
44	    const { config } = options.generator
45	    const outputDir = getOutputPath(options.generator)
46	    const tsconfig = getTsconfig(outputDir)?.config
47	
48	    const target = config.runtime !== undefined ? parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown(config.runtime) : 'nodejs'
49	
50	    const generatedFileExtension =
51	      config.generatedFileExtension !== undefined ? parseGeneratedFileExtension(config.generatedFileExtension) : 'ts'
52	
53	    const importFileExtension =
54	      config.importFileExtension !== undefined
55	        ? parseImportFileExtension(config.importFileExtension)
56	        : inferImportFileExtension({
57	            tsconfig,
58	            generatedFileExtension,
59	            target,
60	          })
61	
62	    const moduleFormat =
63	      config.moduleFormat !== undefined
64	        ? parseModuleFormatFromUnknown(config.moduleFormat)
65	        : inferModuleFormat({
66	            tsconfig,
67	            generatedFileExtension,
68	            importFileExtension,
69	          })
70	
71	    await generateClient({
72	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
73	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
74	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
75	      datasources: options.datasources,
76	      outputDir,
77	      runtimeBase: '@prisma/client/runtime',
78	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
79	      generator: options.generator,
80	      engineVersion: options.version,
81	      clientVersion,
82	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
83	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
84	      target,
85	      generatedFileExtension,
86	      importFileExtension,
87	      moduleFormat,
88	      tsNoCheckPreamble: true, // Set to false only during internal tests
89	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild, target),
90	    })
91	  }
92	}
93	
94	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown, target: RuntimeTargetInternal): 'fast' | 'small' {
95	  if (value === undefined) {
96	    // using the 'small' build for 'vercel-edge' target to fit within their free tier limits
97	    return target === 'vercel-edge' ? 'small' : 'fast'
98	  }
99	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
100	    return value
101	  }
102	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
103	}
104

1	import type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'
2	
3	import { SqlQueryOutput } from './typedSql'
4	
5	export interface GeneratorConfig {
6	  name: string
7	  output: EnvValue | null
8	  isCustomOutput?: boolean
9	  provider: EnvValue
10	  config: {
11	    /** `output` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.output` */
12	    output?: never
13	    /** `provider` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.provider` */
14	    provider?: never
15	    /** `binaryTargets` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.binaryTargets` */
16	    binaryTargets?: never
17	    /** `previewFeatures` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.previewFeatures` */
18	    previewFeatures?: never
19	  } & {
20	    [key: string]: string | string[] | undefined
21	  }
22	  binaryTargets: BinaryTargetsEnvValue[]
23	  // TODO why is this not optional?
24	  previewFeatures: string[]
25	  sourceFilePath: string
26	}
27	
28	export interface EnvValue {
29	  fromEnvVar: null | string
30	  value: null | string
31	}
32	
33	export interface BinaryTargetsEnvValue {
34	  fromEnvVar: string | null
35	  value: string
36	  native?: boolean
37	}
38	
39	export type ConnectorType =
40	  | 'mysql'
41	  | 'mongodb'
42	  | 'sqlite'
43	  | 'postgresql'
44	  | 'postgres' // TODO: we could normalize postgres to postgresql this in engines to reduce the complexity?
45	  | 'prisma+postgres' // Note: used for Prisma Postgres, managed by PDP
46	  | 'sqlserver'
47	  | 'cockroachdb'
48	
49	export type ActiveConnectorType = Exclude<ConnectorType, 'postgres' | 'prisma+postgres'>
50	
51	export interface DataSource {
52	  name: string
53	  provider: ConnectorType
54	  // In Rust, this comes from `Connector::provider_name()`
55	  activeProvider: ActiveConnectorType
56	  schemas: string[] | []
57	  sourceFilePath: string
58	}
59	
60	export type BinaryPaths = {
61	  schemaEngine?: { [binaryTarget: string]: string } // key: target, value: path
62	}
63	
64	/** The options passed to the generator implementations */
65	export type GeneratorOptions = {
66	  generator: GeneratorConfig
67	  // TODO: what is otherGenerators for?
68	  otherGenerators: GeneratorConfig[]
69	  schemaPath: string
70	  dmmf: DMMF.Document
71	  datasources: DataSource[]
72	  // TODO deprecate datamodel & rename to schema?
73	  datamodel: string
74	  // TODO is it really always version hash? Feature is unclear.
75	  version: string // version hash
76	  binaryPaths?: BinaryPaths
77	  noHints?: boolean
78	  allowNoModels?: boolean
79	  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
80	}
81	
82	export type EngineType = 'schemaEngine'
83	
84	export type GeneratorManifest = {
85	  prettyName?: string
86	  defaultOutput?: string
87	  denylists?: {
88	    models?: string[]
89	    fields?: string[]
90	  }
91	  requiresGenerators?: string[]
92	  requiresEngines?: EngineType[]
93	  version?: string
94	  requiresEngineVersion?: string
95	}
96

import { ReadonlyDeep } from './util'

export type Document = ReadonlyDeep<{
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}>

export type Mappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
  otherOperations: {
    read: string[]
    write: string[]
  }
}>

export type OtherOperationMappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  read: string[]
  write: string[]
}>

export type DatamodelEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: EnumValue[]
  dbName?: string | null
  documentation?: string
}>

export type SchemaEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: string[]
}>

export type EnumValue = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
}>

export type Datamodel = ReadonlyDeep<{
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}>

export type uniqueIndex = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: string[]
}>
export type PrimaryKey = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string | null
  fields: string[]
}>
export type Model = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}>

export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'

export type FieldNamespace = 'model' | 'prisma'
export type FieldLocation = 'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'

export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
  kind: FieldKind
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  /**
   * Describes the data type in the same the way it is defined in the Prisma schema:
   * BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, Decimal, Float, Int, JSON, String, $ModelName
   */
  type: string
  /**
   * Native database type, if specified.
   * For example, `@db.VarChar(191)` is encoded as `['VarChar', ['191']]`,
   * `@db.Text` is encoded as `['Text', []]`.
   */
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
